title: Share of one-year-olds vaccinated against measles
subtitle: Share of one-year-olds who have received the first dose of the measles vaccine (MCV1).
caption:
Share of one-year-olds vaccinated against measles, 2024

Share of one-year-olds who have received the first dose of the measles vaccine (MCV1).

This choropleth map displays the global distribution of measles vaccination coverage for one-year-olds in 2024, using a color scale ranging from pale yellow for 0% coverage to deep navy blue for 100% coverage. The highest vaccination rates, represented by the darkest blue shades indicating 95% to 100% coverage, appear across most of North America, including the United States and Canada, as well as throughout Europe, Russia, China, and Australia. In South America, Brazil and Argentina also fall within these highest coverage bands. Conversely, the lowest coverage levels, shown in pale yellow and light green representing the 0% to 60% range, are concentrated in central and western Africa, with countries like Chad, Nigeria, and the Democratic Republic of the Congo visible in these lower bands. Parts of South Asia, including Afghanistan and Pakistan, along with Yemen in the Middle East, also display colors corresponding to the 60% to 80% coverage range. Many nations in South America and Southeast Asia occupy the middle of the spectrum, with shades of teal and medium blue indicating coverage between 80% and 90%. The map includes a "No data" category for regions where information is unavailable, visible as grey hatching in Greenland and parts of central Africa. The visualization shows a distinct geographic pattern where high vaccination coverage is widespread across high-income regions and large emerging economies, while the lowest coverage figures are clustered in specific regions of sub-Saharan Africa and conflict-affected areas in Asia.
